comment: 3-year rolling MLB park factors. hr = home run factor, runs = runs scored factor, hits = hits factor. 1.00 = league average. Updated for 2024 season.

coors_field.wind_notes: High altitude dramatically increases carry on all batted balls

great_american_ballpark.wind_notes: Short dimensions to left-center, warm Ohio summers

yankee_stadium.wind_notes: Short porch in right field (314 ft) massive for LHB

citizens_bank_park.wind_notes: Wind patterns from Delaware River often blow out

wrigley_field.wind_notes: Most wind-dependent park in MLB — check direction before every pick

truist_park.wind_notes: Elevation + warm Georgia weather boosts carry

chase_field.wind_notes: Retractable roof — check if open (heat and elevation amplify) or closed

rogers_centre.wind_notes: Turf plays fast; dome nullifies weather effects

globe_life_field.wind_notes: Climate-controlled dome, Texas heat adds carry in open sections

nationals_park.wind_notes: Wind patterns vary; warmer summer games favor offense

guaranteed_rate_field.wind_notes: Wind similar to Wrigley but less dramatic effect

camden_yards.wind_notes: RF power alley short, humidity summer months

minute_maid_park.wind_notes: Crawford Boxes in LF only 315 ft; retractable roof affects play

tropicana_field.wind_notes: Dome eliminates weather; turf plays fast

american_family_field.wind_notes: Retractable roof; check if open for cold Milwaukee nights

dodger_stadium.wind_notes: Marine layer from Pacific suppresses fly balls on cold nights

angel_stadium.wind_notes: Marine influence moderate; warm days good for offense

target_field.wind_notes: Cold springs depress offense significantly; check temps

progressive_field.wind_notes: Lake Erie wind can suppress HR; watch direction

comerica_park.wind_notes: Deep power alleys, large outfield suppresses HR significantly

kauffman_stadium.wind_notes: Large outfield, pitcher-friendly; wind from south can help

t_mobile_park.wind_notes: Marine air from Puget Sound suppresses fly balls; retractable roof

pnc_park.wind_notes: River valley location, deep outfield dimensions

american_airlines_center.wind_notes: Placeholder — Globe Life Field is current Rangers park

fenway_park.wind_notes: Green Monster suppresses HR but generates wall doubles; many hits

oracle_park.wind_notes: McCovey Cove wind off SF Bay — most suppressive park for HR in MLB

petco_park.wind_notes: Marine layer from Pacific, deep power alleys — most pitcher-friendly park in NL

loanDepot_park.wind_notes: Retractable dome most games due to weather; suppresses offense when closed

citi_field.wind_notes: Flushing winds vary; power alleys deep in CF and RF

notes.altitude_effect: Every 1000 ft above sea level increases ball carry by ~0.5%. Coors Field (5183 ft) has the most dramatic effect.
notes.wind_threshold: Wind speeds above 15 mph have meaningful impact. Direction matters most — check if blowing toward power alleys.
notes.temperature_effect: Cold air is denser and kills carry. Below 50°F reduces HR probability significantly. Above 85°F in direct sunlight adds carry.
notes.wrigley_note: Wrigley Field is uniquely wind-dependent. When blowing out toward Waveland Ave (SW wind), it becomes one of the highest HR parks in MLB. When blowing in off Lake Michigan, it becomes a pitcher's park.
